Our Journey Our Students Projects
Join the Next Batch

Our Projects

Hands-on implementation of real-world applications using modern technologies and AI tools. Each project represents our practical learning journey and technical growth.

LinuxDojo Commands Playground

A simple web-based Linux commands practice tool featuring searchable command lessons with tag filters for rapid learning and hands-on CLI practice in the browser.

Nexus Network Interface

An interactive network intelligence dashboard showing system status, DNS, trace execution, and core metrics with a streamlined console-style UI.

Asciinema Studio

A browser-based .cast editing toolkit for terminal recordings, letting you trim and merge sessions quickly with intuitive controls.

Asciinema Player

A lightweight in-browser player for .cast terminal recordings that supports drag-and-drop playback, speed control, and offline preview.

Telugish Magic Translator

A compact English to Telugu transliteration and translation tool with input/output fields and recent translations history.

BillCraft Professional

A billing and invoice generator with customizable headers, client fields, itemized tables, and PDF export for professional document creation.

City Bus Advisor

A backend-driven application that provides route-based and place-based city bus schedules using FastAPI, SQLite, and a simple web frontend.

Biorefinery Symposium Website

A responsive academic event website designed to showcase symposium details, schedules, speakers, registration information, and venue highlights with a clean and professional layout.

GATOX – Menu Card Project

A simple web-based digital menu page showcasing food and dessert items with a clean layout for easy viewing and restaurant-style presentation.

Smart Dictionary

A lightweight web-based dictionary tool that helps users search and understand word meanings between English and Telugish for quick language reference.

content.thinking:
Both images are in portrait orientation (width < height). I need to convert them to landscape. 

For converting portrait to landscape, the common approach is to rotate 90 degrees. But that might not give the best result if the content is designed to be portrait. Let me think about this...

The user said "convert the given images to landscape" - the most straightforward interpretation is to rotate them 90 degrees clockwise or counter-clockwise to make width > height.

Let me rotate them 90 degrees and save as both JPEG/WebP. But since vamsi-project1.jpeg is missing, I'll work with what we have.
So I have vamsi-project1 in WebP format at 1194x1600 pixels, which I'll rotate to 1600x1194. For vamsi-project2, both JPEG and WebP versions exist at 893x1600, and I'll rotate those to 1600x893. I'll overwrite the existing files with the rotated versions.

content.text: Both images are portrait (taller than wide). I'll rotate them 90° to make them landscape and overwrite all existing versions:

input.command:
python3 -c "
from PIL import Image

# vamsi-project1 (only webp exists)
img1 = Image.open('/home/pavankumar19/aikaryashala.github.io/images/projects/vamsi-project1.webp')
img1_land = img1.rotate(-90, expand=True)
img1_land.save('/home/pavankumar19/aikaryashala.github.io/images/projects/vamsi-project1.webp', 'WEBP')
print(f'vamsi-project1.webp: {img1_land.size}')

# vamsi-project2 (jpeg + webp)
img2 = Image.open('/home/pavankumar19/aikaryashala.github.io/images/projects/vamsi-project2.jpeg')
img2_land = img2.rotate(-90, expand=True)
img2_land.save('/home/pavankumar19/aikaryashala.github.io/images/projects/vamsi-project2.jpeg', 'JPEG', quality=95)
img2_land.save('/home/pavankumar19/aikaryashala.github.io/images/projects/vamsi-project2.webp', 'WEBP')
print(f'vamsi-project2.jpeg/.webp: {img2_land.size}')
"
input.description: Rotate portrait images 90° clockwise to landscape

content.text:
Done. Both images are now landscape:

- `vamsi-project1.webp`: 1600×1194
- `vamsi-project2.jpeg` / `.webp`: 1600×893

If the rotation direction looks wrong when you preview, let me know and I'll flip it the other way.
